headline: Survey at 15% completion; strong regional concentration risks skewing results.
collection_status: 204 of 1,350 responses collected (15% progress). Lindi dominates with 33% of responses; Songwe, Mtwara, and Singida significantly under-represented. Current pace suggests off-track delivery without intervention.
key_findings: Teachers comprise 75% of sample (121/204); senior officials (DEO, REO, ARC) severely under-represented at 4%, limiting district/regional strategic input.
Expand KiuFunza priority leads with 72 mentions, but scale readiness average of 2.8/5 signals critical implementation gaps despite high enthusiasm.

Partner satisfaction strong at 4.5/5, but qualitative feedback emphasizes need for sustained teacher incentives, digital monitoring, and government policy integration—not yet reflected in scaled operations.
Singida (1 response) and Mtwara (12) critically under-resourced; geographic bias toward Lindi-Tanga corridor may miss regional variation in readiness and constraints.
flags: Regional imbalance: Lindi + Tanga account for 54% of responses; Songwe, Mtwara, Singida combined = 4.4%. Risk of unrepresentative findings and missed regional voices.
Senior leadership gap: Only 13 responses from DEO/REO/ARC/REC combined. District and regional coordination priorities unheard; policy scale-up recommendations lack institutional anchoring.

Scale-readiness paradox: 2.8/5 average suggests implementation barriers (incentives, systems, coordination) undermine expansion ambitions. Feedback cites need for digital systems and inter-agency alignment not yet visible in data.
recommendation: Immediately mobilize targeted collection in under-sampled regions (Songwe, Mtwara, Singida) and launch direct outreach to district/regional officials (target: 80+ DEO/REO/ARC responses). Parallel deep-dive on the 2.8 scale-readiness score to surface implementation blockers before expansion planning proceeds.
